mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has been held near the zero lower bound (0.1% as of Feb 2012) for an extended period, compressing the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and pushing investors into risk assets in a hunt for yield. Sustained near-zero FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage_value=0.1 at 2012-02-01 unchanged from prior month
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during the ZIRP period, or forward returns are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window signals accelerating labor market healing, which raises expected consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >= 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 8.5% in late 2011 to 8.3% by Feb 2012
failure_condition: In periods when the trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in less than 55% of instances, or the average triggered return is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional average returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based consumer and housing demand recovery that is not visible from either series alone; this combined demand impulse predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in stronger near-term growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=723.0 vs vintage 699.0 in Jan 2012 indicating upward momentum
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS+HOUST momentum signal fails to outperform either single-series model on out-of-sample Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) acceleration signals expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization. During an early-stage post-recession recovery regime, this predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns as it confirms broadening economic expansion; however, in a late-cycle overheating regime the same acceleration signals inflation and tightening risk, reversing the relationship to negative.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value=96.9318 vs vintage 95.9054 in Jan 2012, indicating output growth
regime_hint: early-stage recovery from recession (distinct from late-cycle expansion)
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces negative or flat 60-day forward SPX returns during confirmed early-recovery regimes (as dated by NBER-style recession exit windows), or produces positive returns during confirmed late-cycle regimes, contradicting the regime-conditional direction flip.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation rises above 2.5%, markets begin pricing in the risk of earlier-than-expected Fed tightening away from the zero lower bound, raising the discount rate on future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=227.842 vs vintage 227.505 for Jan 2012
failure_condition: When the CPI trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or mean triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month gains in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) reflect underlying corporate confidence in demand and translate into sustained near-term buying pressure on equities, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value=133509.0 vs vintage 132697.0 for Feb 2012, showing net job gains
regime_hint: any regime with positive payroll momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, indicating no persistent short-horizon momentum transmission.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) amplify each other: cheap financing accelerates housing construction, which has large multiplier effects on durable goods demand and employment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter that exceed what either signal predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST rising to 723.0 (revised) while FEDFUNDS remains at 0.1% in early 2012
regime_hint: housing recovery under accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction model fails to improve 60-day forward return Rank IC relative to a HOUST-only model over a 2-year rolling out-of-sample window, indicating the interaction effect is spurious.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) occurring during a late-cycle expansion regime signals waning economic momentum and rising recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; the same deceleration during an early-recovery regime is interpreted as noise and has no reliable directional effect.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage_value=13429.9 vs later revised_value=17222.583, reflecting major methodology/level revision risk
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration during confirmed late-cycle regimes fails to produce below-median 20-day forward SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the effect disappears when controlling for GDPC1 high revision risk via placebo tests on pre-revision vintages.
